item.title: An on-chip programmable valley optoelectronic nanocircuit
item.author: Chi LiKaijian XingHaoran Ren
item.content:encoded:
An on-chip light-programmable valley optoelectronic nanocircuit enabled by meta-waveguide photodetectors integrated with transition metal dichalcogenides is demonstrated. In situ generation, selective routing and electrical readout of valley-dependent chiral photons are achieved.

Chi LiKaijian XingHaoran Ren

Nature Photonics

item.title: Nonlinear atomic tunnelling boosted by bright squeezed vacuum
item.author: Zhejun JiangShengzhe PanJian Wu
item.content:encoded:
Bright squeezed vacuum light boosts nonlinear atomic tunnelling ionization more than 20-fold compared with coherent light, enabling quantum control of strong-field processes without increasing classical intensity.

Zhejun JiangShengzhe PanJian Wu

Nature

item.title: Quantum light source boosts attosecond science
item.author: Jan Marcus Dahlström
item.content:encoded:
Ionization experiment shows that quantum light can behave like a conventional laser that has a higher intensity.

Jan Marcus Dahlström

Nature

item.title: The CAPTCHA protocol
item.author: Shiao Wang
item.content:encoded:
Difficult choices.

Shiao Wang

Nature

item.title: A deep-learning framework reveals whole-body perturbations at cell level
item.author: Doris KalteneckerIzabela HorvathAli Ertürk
item.content:encoded:
An analysis of diet-induced obesity using MouseMapper—a suite of foundation-model-based deep-learning algorithms—identifies structural alterations of the infraorbital branch of the trigeminal ganglia.

Doris KalteneckerIzabela HorvathAli Ertürk

Nature

item.title: Cusp-singularity-enhanced Coriolis effect for sensitive chip-scale gyroscopes
item.author: Sen ZhangDingbang XiaoXin Zhou
item.content:encoded:
By using singularity physics to enable cubic-root scaling of frequency and phase modulations induced by the Coriolis effect to enhance the performance of chip-scale Coriolis vibratory gyroscopes, substantial improvements in signal-to-noise ratio and precision are demonstrated.

Sen ZhangDingbang XiaoXin Zhou

Nature

item.title: Free-space twin-field quantum key distribution
item.author: Yu-Huai LiTing ZengJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
By developing frequency locking and time synchronization between independent ultrastable lasers and atomic clocks, twin-field quantum key distribution is demonstrated over 14.2-km turbulent free-space channels, with a secure key rate exceeding the repeaterless capacity bound.

Yu-Huai LiTing ZengJian-Wei Pan

Nature Photonics

item.title: Entanglement-enabled image transmission through complex media
item.author: Chloé VernièreRaphaël GuitterHugo Defienne
item.content:encoded:
Classical approaches to imaging through complex media do not account for the quantum nature of the incident field. Now, images encoded on an entangled two-photon state are shown to transmit through a scattering medium whereas scattered by classical light.

Chloé VernièreRaphaël GuitterHugo Defienne

Nature Physics

item.title: Gaussian boson sampling with 1,024 squeezed states in 8,176 modes
item.author: Hua-Liang LiuHao SuJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
A programmable photonic quantum processor, Jiuzhang 4.0, incorporates 1,024 high-efficiency squeezed states into a hybrid spatial–temporal encoded 8,176-mode circuit.

Hua-Liang LiuHao SuJian-Wei Pan

Nature

item.title: Mesoscale atomic engineering in a crystal lattice
item.author: Julian KleinKevin M. RoccaprioreFrances M. Ross
item.content:encoded:
Electron-beam control enables deterministic placement of tens of thousands of atomic defects in three-dimensional crystals, creating stable, programmable artificial matter for scalable quantum and nanoscale technologies.

Julian KleinKevin M. RoccaprioreFrances M. Ross

Nature

item.title: Targeted electron beam creates thousands of atomic crystal defects
item.author: Toma Susi
item.content:encoded:
An electron-beam technique that can precisely create thousands of atomic defects in a crystal could be used to build quantum devices.

Toma Susi

Nature

item.title: Laser mode braiding on a chip
item.author: Wenbo MaoBofeng ZhuLan Yang
item.content:encoded:
Non-Hermitian systems support non-trivial topological effects, yet eigenvalue braiding remains difficult to control and observe. Now, active tuning of laser modes enables programmable and directly observable braiding on an integrated photonic chip.

Wenbo MaoBofeng ZhuLan Yang

Nature Physics

item.title: Higher-order harmonics in Josephson tunnel junctions due to series inductance
item.author: Junghyun KimMax HaysWilliam D. Oliver
item.content:encoded:
Deviations from the textbook current–phase relationship of a Josephson junction can arise from the intrinsic physics of the junction, but also from the inductance of metallic traces. Now a scheme has been developed to distinguish these cases.

Junghyun KimMax HaysWilliam D. Oliver

Nature Physics

item.title: Daily briefing: The cities getting ‘richer and cleaner’
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of cities worldwide are nailing green growth without fossil fuels. Plus, why the common hormonal condition PCOS is now PMOS and how to vibe code in science.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Scalable generation of massive Schrödinger cat states via quantum tunnelling
item.author: Han ZhangYong-Kui WangBing Yang
item.content:encoded:
Massive spatial superpositions are a resource for quantum interferometry, but it has been hard to generate them beyond single atoms. Now spatially entangled massive states are realized through the tunnelling of atomic clusters in optical lattices.

Han ZhangYong-Kui WangBing Yang

Nature Physics

item.title: A metropolitan-scale multiplexed quantum repeater with Bell non-locality
item.author: Tian-Xiang ZhuChao ZhangGuang-Can Guo
item.content:encoded:
Time-division multiplexing in a metropolitan-scale quantum repeater is demonstrated. Heralded entanglement between remote quantum memories is implemented over a distance of 14.5 km, generating a Bell state with a fidelity of 78.6% with a Bell inequality violation of S = 2.22.

Tian-Xiang ZhuChao ZhangGuang-Can Guo

Nature Photonics

item.title: Ytterbium spectroscopy for new physics searches
item.author: Julian Berengut
item.content:encoded:
Highly precise measurements of inner-shell transitions in ytterbium atoms can help test our understanding of physics and the existence and properties of new fundamental particles.

Julian Berengut

Nature Photonics

item.title: Two-qubit logic and teleportation with mobile spin qubits in silicon
item.author: Y. MatsumotoM. De SmetL. M. K. Vandersypen
item.content:encoded:
A device architecture comprising silicon-based mobile qubits and the shuttling of two electron spins together demonstrates two-qubit gate fidelity of about 99% as well as quantum state teleportation between qubits separated by 320 nm.

Y. MatsumotoM. De SmetL. M. K. Vandersypen

Nature

item.title: Nanoscale ‘conveyor belt’ teleports quantum state of electron
item.author: Lars R. Schreiber
item.content:encoded:
Mobile quantum bits, which can be shuttled to where they are needed in a circuit, have been used to perform a quantum process called state teleportation.

Lars R. Schreiber

Nature

item.title: Quantum coherent manipulation and readout of superconducting vortex states
item.author: Ameya NambisanSimon GünzlerIoan M. Pop
item.content:encoded:
Vortices trapped in superconducting granular aluminium films can behave as a quantum two-level system that can be manipulated and read out, suggesting that they could be used for future quantum technologies.

Ameya NambisanSimon GünzlerIoan M. Pop

Nature

item.title: Purcell-enhanced spin–phonon coupling with a single colour centre
item.author: Graham JoeMichael HaasMarko Lončar
item.content:encoded:
The acoustic Purcell effect is observed by constructing a specially engineered, microwave-frequency nanomechanical resonator around a colour centre spin qubit in diamond.

Graham JoeMichael HaasMarko Lončar

Nature

item.title: In twisted graphene, some electrons are heavier than others
item.author: Hongyun ZhangShuyun Zhou
item.content:encoded:
When two graphene layers are twisted at a ‘magic angle’, their electrons can behave as both heavy and light particles, depending on momentum.

Hongyun ZhangShuyun Zhou

Nature

item.title: Quantum random access memory put to the test
item.author: Connor T. Hann
item.content:encoded:
Specialized quantum memories will be required to achieve quantum speedups for data-intensive problems. Now, a proof-of-principle demonstration of such a quantum memory has been performed with a superconducting processor.

Connor T. Hann

Nature Physics

item.title: Squeezed, trisqueezed and quadsqueezed states via spin–oscillator coupling
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
A method applied to a single trapped ion combines two linear spin-dependent interactions to generate nonlinear couplings in the ion’s motion: squeezing, trisqueezing and quadsqueezing interactions are demonstrated. The approach can be applied to any spin–oscillator system, produces stronger unitary interactions with the flexibility to switch quickly between orders, and scales seamlessly to higher orders and multiple oscillators.

Nature Physics

item.title: Quantum ‘thermometer’ takes temperatures inside living cancer cells
item.author: Matthew Hutson
item.content:encoded:
Nanosensors showed that different parts of the cell varied by as much as 1 ºC.

Matthew Hutson

Nature

item.title: Squeezing, trisqueezing and quadsqueezing in a hybrid oscillator–spin system
item.author: O. BăzăvanS. SanerR. Srinivas
item.content:encoded:
Higher-order interactions in quantum harmonic oscillator systems can result in useful effects, but they are hard to engineer. An experiment on a single trapped ion now demonstrates how spin can mediate higher-order nonlinear bosonic interactions.

O. BăzăvanS. SanerR. Srinivas

Nature Physics

item.title: Coherence of a hole-spin flopping-mode qubit in a circuit quantum electrodynamics environment
item.author: Léo NoirotCécile X. YuSimon Zihlmann
item.content:encoded:
Coupling semiconductor qubit devices to microwave resonators provides a way to transfer quantum information over long distances. A flopping-mode qubit that combines strong coupling to photons with good coherence properties has now been demonstrated.

Léo NoirotCécile X. YuSimon Zihlmann

Nature Physics

item.title: Digital quantum magnetism on a trapped-ion quantum computer
item.author: R. HaghshenasE. ChertkovM. Foss-Feig
item.content:encoded:
Simulations performed using Quantinuum’s H2 trapped-ion quantum computer for observing thermalization on challenging timescales demonstrate the usefulness of digital quantum computers for investigating continuous-time dynamics in regimes that classical simulation methods find difficult.

R. HaghshenasE. ChertkovM. Foss-Feig

Nature

item.title: Author Correction: Quantum error correction below the surface code threshold
item.author: Rajeev AcharyaDmitry A. AbaninNicholas Zobrist
item.content:encoded:
Rajeev AcharyaDmitry A. AbaninNicholas Zobrist

Nature

item.title: Exceptional deficiency of non-Hermitian systems
item.author: Zhen LiRundong CaiGuancong Ma
item.content:encoded:
Exceptional points and their related phenomena are limited to a narrow bandwidth and require precise control to realize. Now a high-dimensional generalization of exceptional points is shown to enable broadband non-Hermitian dynamics.

Zhen LiRundong CaiGuancong Ma

Nature Physics

item.title: Integrated photonics for continuous-variable quantum optics
item.author: Rachel N. ClarkBethany PuzioJonathan C. F. Matthews
item.content:encoded:
In this Review the authors cover the latest efforts to integrate sources and detectors of continuous-variable quantum light states into chip-scale photonic integrated circuits.

Rachel N. ClarkBethany PuzioJonathan C. F. Matthews

Nature Photonics

item.title: Planckian scattering and parallel conduction channels in an iron chalcogenide superconductor
item.author: Ralph Romero IIIHee Taek YiN. P. Armitage
item.content:encoded:
The link between the strange metal with its linear-in-temperature resistivity and superconductivity is ambiguous. Now, a channel in the normal state whose scattering rate is linear in temperature is shown to drive superconductivity in FeTe1−xSex.

Ralph Romero IIIHee Taek YiN. P. Armitage

Nature Physics

item.title: Field-induced superconductivity in a magnetically doped two-dimensional crystal
item.author: Adrian LlanosVeronica ShowJoseph Falson
item.content:encoded:
Magnetic fields usually weaken superconductivity. By contrast, a material platform is demonstrated where applying a moderate field induces superconductivity.

Adrian LlanosVeronica ShowJoseph Falson

Nature Physics

item.title: Glycerol-driven TNAP activation in thermogenesis and mineralization
item.author: Mohammed Faiz HussainShreya S. KrishnanLawrence Kazak
item.content:encoded:
Glycerol is an allosteric activator of tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNAP), activating the futile creatine cycle.

Mohammed Faiz HussainShreya S. KrishnanLawrence Kazak

Nature

item.title: Two-electron quantum walks for probing entanglement and decoherence in an electron microscope
item.author: Offek TzipermanDavid NabbenPeter Baum
item.content:encoded:
Entanglement between particles offers insights into quantum behaviour, but methods for studying it in free-electron systems are lacking. Now a two-electron quantum walk is used to probe decoherence of free electrons inside an electron microscope.

Offek TzipermanDavid NabbenPeter Baum

Nature Physics

item.title: Average topological phase in a disordered Rydberg atom array
item.author: Zongpei YueYu-Feng MaoLi You
item.content:encoded:
In addition to strongly protected topological phases that rely on exact symmetries, theory predicts that disorder can stabilize weakly protected phases in mixed quantum states, and an example of the latter is now observed in a Rydberg atom array.

Zongpei YueYu-Feng MaoLi You

Nature Physics

item.title: Inter-magnet pumping counters dissipation in artificial ferrimagnets
item.author: Kai ZhangY. X. NiuJ. Li
item.content:encoded:
Reducing dissipation when generating spin currents remains a central challenge in spintronics. Now, an artificial ferrimagnet is shown to produce spin current output and simultaneously lower magnetic damping.

Kai ZhangY. X. NiuJ. Li

Nature Physics

item.title: Daily briefing: Is a nine-to-five PhD possible?
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
PhD students share how they make their doctorate fit into the hours of a standard working week. Plus, male and female brain cells differ in gene activity and a whole new way to produce DNA.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Quantum computers take on health care: light-sensitive cancer drugs win US$2-million contest
item.author: Elizabeth Gibney
item.content:encoded:
Quantum machines are making inroads into biology but have no ‘advantage’ over classical machines yet.

Elizabeth Gibney

Nature

item.title: Efficient thermalization and universal quantum computing with quantum Gibbs samplers
item.author: Cambyse RouzéDaniel Stilck FrançaÁlvaro M. Alhambra
item.content:encoded:
Quantum simulation of equilibrium many-body systems requires the ability to sample from the thermal distribution of quantum states. An algorithm has now been proven to be an appropriate quantum analogue to classical Monte Carlo methods.

Cambyse RouzéDaniel Stilck FrançaÁlvaro M. Alhambra

Nature Physics

item.title: Intrinsic phononic dressed states in a nanomechanical system
item.author: M. YukselM. P. MaksymowychM. L. Roukes
item.content:encoded:
Exploiting the differences between quantum and classical physics typically requires nonlinear devices. Quantum nonlinear effects have now been demonstrated in a nanomechanical resonator due to strong coupling with an intrinsic two-level defect.

M. YukselM. P. MaksymowychM. L. Roukes

Nature Physics

item.title: NSF awards record number of coveted PhD fellowships in surprise move
item.author: Dan Garisto
item.content:encoded:
Quantum science and AI research are big winners just a year after the US funding giant slashed its Graduate Research Fellowship Program awards in half.

Dan Garisto

Nature

item.title: High-fidelity collisional quantum gates with fermionic atoms
item.author: Petar BojovićTimon HilkerTitus Franz
item.content:encoded:
A robust composite pair-exchange gate based on controlled interactions of fermionic atoms in an optical superlattice demonstrates high fidelities and long Bell-state lifetimes, marking an important step towards a fully digital fermionic quantum computer.

Petar BojovićTimon HilkerTitus Franz

Nature

item.title: Protected quantum gates using qubit doublons in dynamical optical lattices
item.author: Yann KieferZijie ZhuTilman Esslinger
item.content:encoded:
A purely geometric two-qubit SWAP gate can be realized by transiently populating qubit doublon states of fermionic atoms in a dynamical optical lattice.

Yann KieferZijie ZhuTilman Esslinger

Nature

item.title: Microcombs for the generation of tunable structured microwaves
item.author: Yuanhua FengXuebing Zhang
item.content:encoded:
Structured microwaves emitted by a chip-based microcomb source equipped with an antenna array enable ultrahigh-capacity communications and multi-variable sensing.

Yuanhua FengXuebing Zhang

Nature Photonics

item.title: High-precision measurement of the W boson mass with the CMS experiment
item.author: V. ChekhovskyA. HayrapetyanD. Druzhkin
item.content:encoded:
The CMS experiment at CERN reports one of the highest-precision measurements of the W boson mass, finding it in line with standard model predictions and at odds with recent anomalous measurements.

V. ChekhovskyA. HayrapetyanD. Druzhkin

Nature

item.title: Quantum ground-state cooling of two librational modes of a nanorotor
item.author: Stephan TroyerFlorian FechtelMarkus Arndt
item.content:encoded:
Controlling the rotational motion of nanoscale objects by trapping and cooling is a prerequisite for exploring quantum rotational phenomena. Now, two orthogonal librational modes of a levitated nanorotor are cooled into their quantum ground state.

Stephan TroyerFlorian FechtelMarkus Arndt

Nature Physics

item.title: All-optical polarization control in time-varying low-index films via plasma symmetry breaking
item.author: Wallace JaffraySven StengelMarcello Ferrera
item.content:encoded:
Femtosecond pumping of an isotropic low-index subwavelength film enables ultrafast polarization control, with induced birefringence up to 0.1π μm−1, a dichroic ratio of ~23% and a non-reciprocal optical activity of ±1.1° μm−1.

Wallace JaffraySven StengelMarcello Ferrera

Nature Photonics

item.title: Evidence for odd-parity superconductivity underpinned by antiferromagnetism in heavy-fermion metal YbRh2Si2
item.author: Lev V. LevitinJan KnappJohn Saunders
item.content:encoded:
Odd-parity spin-triplet superconductivity remains difficult to establish experimentally. Now, several distinct magnetic-field-tuned superconducting states—including a possible topological helical phase—have been identified in YbRh2Si2.

Lev V. LevitinJan KnappJohn Saunders

Nature Physics

item.title: Massive budget cuts for US science proposed again by Trump administration
item.author: Max KozlovDan GaristoEdward Chen
item.content:encoded:
The 2027 budget proposal would curb federal payments for scientific publishing and reduce funding for many US institutions.

Max KozlovDan GaristoEdward Chen

Nature
